title: Anil adscendens
taxonomy: Anil adscendens is classified as a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is commonly found in the Himalayan foothills. This plant is also known as the 'Indian cordia' and is often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties. Anil adscendens is a deciduous shrub that grows up to 3-4 meters in height, with green leaves and white or pink flowers. The classification of Anil adscendens is further divided into the genus Cordia, with the species being classified as Cordia adscendens.
